evidence_quotes.quote: video game publishers stopping or preventing access to video games.
Being able to play the game in a playable state, perhaps without some of the online function.

paragraphs.text: Hi, I would like to suggest/request that material from stop destroying videogames initiative should be included in the digital fairness act. That has gathered over 1 million signatures concerning the troubled trend of video game publishers stopping or preventing access to video games. With no clear definition if the games are a service or an item purchased. It is my belief that a purchased item should be in the control of the buyer and not the seller. If it's a service it's often not given enough information in advance concerning the closure of the game. There is no clear definition or law on this matter and the seller in question can do whatever they want regardless of the concern of the buyer. We in return don't have a clear definition of what being done is legal or not. Im full in support of the digital fairness act concerning addictive design and personalised targeting. I think that also should include a more fair definition and alternatives to end of life of a game. Being able to play the game in a playable state, perhaps without some of the online function. Since its the belief that this is a right thing for the consumer to be able to do. This is the part of the stop destroying videogames initiative that I want to succeed. Since I want to be able to own my games and be at the mercy of the seller if I do or not at the end of the day.
